sections.paragraph: f. (root נוּף see Hiph. No. 2), a dropping down, dripping, hence נֹפֶת צוּפִים dropping of honeycombs, i.e. honey dropping from the combs, i.q. יַעַר (which see), Psa 19:11. Hence without the gen. צוּפִים id. Son 4:11 Pro 5:3 24:13 , נֹפֶת מָתֹוק עַל־חִכִּךָ “honey droppings (which are) sweet to thy palate,” (where the predicate מָתֹוק is not inflected; compare Gen 49:15).
